mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving demand for construction, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where HOUST rises > 2% monthly despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative SPX returns over 60 trading days due to recessionary expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 60% of INDPRO decline periods or Rank IC > 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 5.5%) combined with rising payrolls (PAYEMS > 150k) signals inflationary pressure, causing SPX returns to turn negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both UNRATE < 5.5% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY) forces central banks to raise rates, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY or Rank IC > 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer demand (RSAFS > 600B) combined with rising GDP (GDPC1 > 2% QoQ) creates a positive feedback loop for corporate profits, driving SPX returns higher over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS > 600B and GDPC1 > 2% QoQ.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 0.5pp in 1 month) signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative SPX returns over 5 trading days due to recession fears.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 100) indicates weak supply-side activity, creating deflationary risks that push SPX returns negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of INDPRO < 100 periods or Rank IC > 0.03.
